ocr.text: In the context of the ongoing assessment of catastrophe loss reserves, this report presents a detailed analysis of the current
financial standing of Berkshire Hathaway Re across various geographical regions. The attached table delineates the distribution
of catastrophe loss reserves, emphasizing the significant disparities observed between the West Coast and the Midwest, where
the West Coast accounts for 15% of the total reserves while the Midwest represents a notably smaller 5%. This variance
underscores the heightened susceptibility to catastrophic events in certain regions, prompting a strategic recalibration of our risk
assessment methodologies.

Additionally, we highlight the International segment, which currently holds reserves amounting to $50 million, reflecting our
commitment to maintaining robust financial support for global operations amidst escalating climate-related risks. The Total
Change metric of 20% further illustrates the dynamic adjustments being made to our catastrophe reserves, a necessary response
to the evolving landscape of insurance liabilities. As we move into 2024, the Market Overview section will further contextualize
these figures within the broader industry trends, ensuring that stakeholders are equipped with a comprehensive understanding of
our positioning and future outlook in the face of increasing natural disaster occurrences.
